commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7ee32ea09935c3968be700ed9a76d110887b5cff)
commands.review_summary:
The PR suppresses exact `REPLY_SKIP` and `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` replies in isolated cron announce delivery and adds core-channel direct-delivery regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +28. Total +40 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main normalizes only `NO_REPLY` in cron direct delivery, so exact `REPLY_SKIP` or `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` text would continue into the outbound send path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus a shared-helper repair decision; missing proof makes this unsuitable for ClawSweeper repair markers right now.

target.title: fix(cron): suppress control replies in announce delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat REPLY_SKIP and ANNOUNCE_SKIP as suppressed control replies in isolated cron announce delivery.
- Suppress those tokens before direct outbound sends and transcript/awareness mirroring, matching the existing NO_REPLY path.

Fixes #85421.

## Verification
Behavior addressed: isolated cron announce delivery does not post literal REPLY_SKIP or ANNOUNCE_SKIP to configured channel targets.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree on Linux Node 22.22.2.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
Evidence after fix: isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts passed, 21 tests.
Observed result after fix: core-channel direct cron announce delivery reports deliveryAttempted=true, delivered=false, and does not call outbound send for REPLY_SKIP or ANNOUNCE_SKIP.
What was not tested: live Discord/Telegram cron execution against real channel credentials.
target.author: clawsean

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
